content:
The bell above the door chimed, a thin, tinny sound that usually cut through the heavy air of the shop, but today it felt muffled, as if the humidity of the city was trying to swallow the noise. I didn’t look up immediately. I was finishing the line of salt, my thumb pressing the grains into the groove of the wooden threshold with a pressure I recognized as my mother’s.
